author: pk910
content:
> and also like potuz is saying you can prove anything from any given block root so if you need to peek back into some history that isn't immediately reachable via the 4788 contract, you'd just extend the proof by a hash or two

it needs 22 hashes to go from one block root to another via the state/block_Roots field 😄
traversing back the parent_root chain in beacon block headers needs 3 hashes per block.

so yea,  proving withdrawals should still work (via the state),   but the proofs are like twice the size when going via the state

author: pk910
content:
what we're actually loosing with ePBS is the ability to prove anything from the ExecutionPayload (or envelope).
There's no envelope or payload root for these containers included in the state or the block body / bid.

Not sure if anyone uses that for anything tho.
Would make more sense when transactions are in SSZ format in future too.

author: ralexstokes
content:
yeah, if someone can show the extra machinery is somehow cost prohibitive then we can re-evaluate the EIP/APIs

but for example there won't be any withdrawals in (execution) empty slots so its a non-issue

author: ralexstokes
content:
> e actually loosing with ePBS is the ability to prove anything from the ExecutionPayload (or envelope).
i'd have to go confirm and about to step away from computer but i think you can get what you need (yes with a slightly increased cost) via the execution bid

author: ralexstokes
content: or otherwise just the state at some point

author: pk910
content: but we're not only missing block roots of empty blocks.  we're missing the parent roots of empty blocks,   so blocks that have withdrawals and co.

author: ralexstokes
content:
right and then i see two options:
* traverse the state (with some higher cost)
* change the engine API so that from the EL's perspective there are no empty blocks

author: pk910
content:
I agree, It's still possible to get these missed block roots proved - by going via the parent_roots (+3 hashes per empty block) or block_roots in state (+22 hashes static).
I think the consumers have to give us feedback if that's too much

author: ralexstokes
content: yeah <@935504619554107392> i may have been too heavy-handed with "no impact", so if you have some time and want to look more closely let's do that

author: ralexstokes
content:
> parent_roots (+3 hashes per empty block) or block_roots in state (+22 hases static).
my intuition here is that gas is cheap enough for this to not be a blocker, but would be good to confirm
